name: File Created with Right to Left Override
notes:
This rule triggers when the "Right to Left Override"  character (U+202E) is found in the name of a created file. In some operating systems, the graphical interface will process this character - reversing the display order of parts of the filename.

For example, the shell script "Grap{U=202E}pdf.sh" may be displayed by the operating system as "Graphs.pdf", which is a filename a user may be more likely to trust.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created
when the event matches Encoded Filename (custom) is not N/A
when the event matches Encoded Filename (custom) contains any of E280AE
TD_list: APPLY File Created with Right to Left Override on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created
AND when the event matches Encoded Filename (custom) is not N/A
AND when the event matches Encoded Filename (custom) contains any of E280AE
RED_list: Event name: File Created with Right to Left Override
Event description: Detects when the "Right to Left Override character" (U+202E) is found in a file name.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
